finding: Surface area underestimated: 82 TS2345 errors span 14 tool source files (Def R generic fix), 6 Schema.Literal files, app-runtime.ts, coordination.ts (error channel), 3 v2/session.ts (unrelated), ~64 test file errors across ~18 files (Layer R channel). Plan acknowledges "tests will need changes" but doesn't enumerate test files or provide strategy for the ~64 test-layer errors.
repair_path: Add a test-file section enumerating the ~18 affected test files. Separate Layer R channel issues from tool Def R generic issues. Consider whether Layer R errors can be fixed with `providing` or a targeted cast similar to app-runtime.

finding: coordination.ts(506) has `DatabaseError` in the init Effect's error channel â€” the outer `Effect.gen(function*() { const adapter = yield* DatabaseAdapter.Service; yield* ensureCoordinationTables(); return { execute: ... } })` returns `Effect<..., DatabaseError, ...>` but define() expects `Effect<..., never, R>`. The R generic on Def only addresses the service channel; the error channel mismatch in coordination.ts will NOT be fixed. The inner execute already has `.pipe(Effect.orDie)` but the outer init effect does not.
repair_path: Wrap `ensureCoordinationTables()` call in init effect with `.pipe(Effect.orDie)` in coordination.ts, or add `.pipe(Effect.orDie)` to the entire init Effect. This converts DatabaseError to never before it hits define().

finding: The plan proposes `R2` as the execute services generic with default `R2 = never`. When TypeScript diagnostics cite `R2` in error messages, developers will see `R2` rather than `ExecuteServices` or `ToolServices`. This reduces error clarity vs. more descriptive naming like `RInit`/`RExec` or `R`/`RTool`.
repair_path: Rename `R2` to `RTool` or `RExec` in the define() signature. This improves the readability of compiler errors and code navigation.

finding: Test file cascade: ~64 TS2345 errors across ~18 test files (tui.test, control-plane/workspace.test, debug-bisect, debug-db-adapter, app-runtime-logger.test, httpapi-*.test (5 files), session tests (4 files), tool tests (7 files)). The plan acknowledges "tests will need changes" but provides no enumeration, no strategy, and no estimate. Many are `Layer<..., never, Service | Service>` not `Layer<..., never, never>` â€” a different variant of the same invariant issue that won't be fixed by the Def R generic alone.
repair_path: Add a test-file section listing all ~18 affected test files. Separate into: (a) tests that mix layers with different R channels â€” need `Layer.provideMerge` or `.pipe(Layer.provide(...))` chain; (b) tests that construct tool `Info`/`Def` directly â€” need to match the new R generic. At minimum file a follow-up issue for the Layer R errors if they're out of scope.
